vocabulary.en: a test that uses a single factor to decide whether something is acceptable or not
vocabulary.excerpt: The Democratic litmus test has been...
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次选举是对民主的试金石。

vocabulary.en: the basic or fundamental problem
vocabulary.excerpt: I think people should be clear as to what exactly they're regarding as the original sin here.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个项目的原罪是缺乏规划。

vocabulary.en: to join a discussion or give an opinion
vocabulary.excerpt: The two categories of people who really need to weigh in are...
vocabulary.example_cn: 专家们就这个问题发表了意见。

vocabulary.en: affected by many scandals
vocabulary.excerpt: This campaign has been so scandal-plagued that I think almost the effect has been watered down.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这位丑闻缠身的政客辞职了。

vocabulary.en: to make something less strong or effective
vocabulary.excerpt: This campaign has been so scandal-plagued that I think almost the effect has been watered down.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们淡化了报告中的批评。

vocabulary.en: the practice of responding to an accusation by making a similar accusation against the accuser
vocabulary.excerpt: There's going to be a lot of whataboutism.
vocabulary.example_cn: 辩论中充满了转移话题。

vocabulary.en: to change an earlier statement or opinion
vocabulary.excerpt: Business mogul Kevin O'Leary backtracking from plans to build a massive A.I. data center in Utah.
vocabulary.example_cn: 政府在其政策上退缩了。

vocabulary.en: safety measures or limits
vocabulary.excerpt: We need some guardrails.
vocabulary.example_cn: 人工智能需要监管护栏。

vocabulary.en: to reproduce itself
vocabulary.excerpt: Because it can self-replicate.
vocabulary.example_cn: 病毒能够自我复制。

vocabulary.en: a speech given at a graduation ceremony
vocabulary.excerpt: Scott Pelley, delivering the commencement address at Wake Forest University just last year.
vocabulary.example_cn: 校长发表了毕业典礼演讲。

vocabulary.en: to dismiss someone from a job or to reduce something
vocabulary.excerpt: She axed several show staffers and correspondents.
vocabulary.example_cn: 公司裁减了数百个工作岗位。

vocabulary.en: to end a relationship or association
vocabulary.excerpt: We had to part ways.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们决定分道扬镳。

vocabulary.en: involving a lot of noise, excitement, activity, or violence
vocabulary.excerpt: These have been the most tumultuous eight days in "60 Minutes" history.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是一个动荡的时期。

vocabulary.en: old and no longer used or suitable for modern times
vocabulary.excerpt: She believes "60 Minutes" is out of date and archaic.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个系统已经过时了。

vocabulary.en: to try to become friendly with someone, especially to gain an advantage
vocabulary.excerpt: Paramount has tried to cozy up to Trump while trying to buy CNN.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他试图讨好老板。

vocabulary.en: in a way that cannot be avoided
vocabulary.excerpt: Those corporate politics are inescapably part of this story.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们不可避免地卷入了冲突。

vocabulary.en: to blame or hold responsible
vocabulary.excerpt: Bondi Pins Epstein File Release On Blanche
vocabulary.example_cn: 她把失败归咎于队友。

vocabulary.en: a hearing to confirm a nominee
vocabulary.excerpt: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche will face a Senate confirmation hearing
vocabulary.example_cn: 法官的确认听证会定于下周举行。

vocabulary.en: private, not open to the public
vocabulary.excerpt: Pam Bondi's closed-door interview with lawmakers
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们举行了一次闭门会议。

vocabulary.en: an easy opportunity
vocabulary.excerpt: Democrats can essentially have a lay-up
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个问题对他们来说是个轻松得分的机会。

vocabulary.en: the act of turning something into a weapon
vocabulary.excerpt: the weaponization fund
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们批评将司法系统武器化。

vocabulary.en: done badly or clumsily
vocabulary.excerpt: this botched rollout
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次搞砸的发布损害了他们的声誉。

vocabulary.en: a decisive test
vocabulary.excerpt: a litmus test on transparency
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次投票是对他忠诚度的试金石。

vocabulary.en: gradually destroy
vocabulary.excerpt: that erodes trust
vocabulary.example_cn: 不断的谎言侵蚀了公众的信任。

vocabulary.en: to calm or satisfy
vocabulary.excerpt: that constituency is not going to be appeased
vocabulary.example_cn: 政府试图安抚抗议者。

vocabulary.en: to sacrifice someone for personal gain
vocabulary.excerpt: throwing him under the bus
vocabulary.example_cn: 为了保住自己的工作，她把同事推出去当替罪羊。

vocabulary.en: to experience false perceptions
vocabulary.excerpt: hallucinated by artificial intelligence
vocabulary.example_cn: 人工智能可能会产生幻觉并编造事实。

vocabulary.en: to impose a penalty
vocabulary.excerpt: getting sanctioned and barred from the court
vocabulary.example_cn: 该律师因不当行为受到制裁。

vocabulary.en: to invent falsely
vocabulary.excerpt: fabricating entire briefs with A.I.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被指控捏造证据。

vocabulary.en: a great flood or overwhelming amount
vocabulary.excerpt: the courts are deluge with these
vocabulary.example_cn: 法院被大量诉讼淹没。

vocabulary.en: an unexpected problem
vocabulary.excerpt: another snag for L.A.
vocabulary.example_cn: 计划遇到了一个障碍。

vocabulary.en: to get off an aircraft
vocabulary.excerpt: They're deplaning
vocabulary.example_cn: 乘客正在下飞机。

vocabulary.en: to push forcefully
vocabulary.excerpt: our senator being jammed to the ground
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被推倒在地。

vocabulary.en: to grab or take quickly
vocabulary.excerpt: if I get snatched up by ICE
vocabulary.example_cn: 移民官员可能会在街上抓人。

vocabulary.en: pricing that changes based on demand
vocabulary.excerpt: the issue is dynamic pricing
vocabulary.example_cn: 机票采用动态定价。

vocabulary.en: to delay legislation by prolonged speaking
vocabulary.excerpt: a secretary who was filibustering
vocabulary.example_cn: 参议员通过冗长发言阻挠议案通过。

vocabulary.en: angry or upset
vocabulary.excerpt: he was obviously triggered
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的评论让她很生气。

vocabulary.en: a fund used for illicit purposes
vocabulary.excerpt: a $1.8 billion slush fund
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们被指控设立贿赂基金。

vocabulary.en: to divert or take away
vocabulary.excerpt: money being siphoned from the sale
vocabulary.example_cn: 资金被从项目中抽走。

vocabulary.en: a line of dancers in a musical
vocabulary.excerpt: cut from a chorus line
vocabulary.example_cn: 他跳舞像合唱队成员。

vocabulary.en: diversity, equity, and inclusion
vocabulary.excerpt: because of DEI
vocabulary.example_cn: 公司致力于DEI倡议。

vocabulary.en: to be forced out due to high costs
vocabulary.excerpt: I hope Elmo gets priced out
vocabulary.example_cn: 许多居民因房价上涨而被挤出市场。

vocabulary.en: origins or background
vocabulary.excerpt: don't you dare forget your roots
vocabulary.example_cn: 他从未忘记自己的根源。

vocabulary.en: a channel or process
vocabulary.excerpt: from YouTube to Hollywood pipeline
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个人才渠道为科技公司输送员工。

vocabulary.en: requiring great strength or effort
vocabulary.excerpt: management of this Herculean task
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是一项艰巨的任务。

vocabulary.en: perfect, without fault
vocabulary.excerpt: his ethics are beyond reproach
vocabulary.example_cn: 她的行为无可指责。

sentences.en: The U.S. Senate took almost 20 hours voting down amendment after amendment.
sentences.cn: 美国参议院花了近20个小时逐一否决修正案。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'voting down'为现在分词短语作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：The U.S. Senate；谓语：took；宾语：almost 20 hours；'voting down amendment after amendment'为现在分词短语，表示伴随动作。

sentences.en: But Senate Republicans rejected proposals to formally kill the president's anti-weaponization fund in the process.
sentences.cn: 但参议院共和党人拒绝了在此过程中正式废除总统反武器化基金的提议。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，不定式短语'to formally kill...'作定语修饰'proposals'。
sentences.analysis: 主语：Senate Republicans；谓语：rejected；宾语：proposals；不定式短语'to formally kill...'修饰'proposals'，说明提议的内容。

sentences.en: Now, just minutes later, seven Senate Republicans voted to block consideration of a contentious bill that would reauthorize the nation's spy powers.
sentences.cn: 几分钟后，七名参议院共和党人投票阻止审议一项有争议的法案，该法案将重新授权国家的间谍权力。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'that would reauthorize...'为定语从句修饰'bill'。
sentences.analysis: 主语：seven Senate Republicans；谓语：voted；宾语：to block consideration of a contentious bill；'that would reauthorize...'为定语从句，修饰'bill'。

sentences.en: A report published in "The New York Times," describing volatile and toxic relationships.
sentences.cn: 《纽约时报》发表的一篇报道描述了动荡且有害的关系。
sentences.structure: 名词短语，'published in...'和'describing...'均为分词短语作定语。
sentences.analysis: 中心词：A report；'published in "The New York Times"'为过去分词短语作定语；'describing volatile and toxic relationships'为现在分词短语作定语。

sentences.en: One of them accuses him of twisting her arm.
sentences.cn: 其中一人指控他扭伤了她的手臂。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾宾补结构，'of twisting her arm'为介词短语作宾语补足语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：One of them；谓语：accuses；宾语：him；'of twisting her arm'为介词短语，补充说明指控的内容。

sentences.en: Some accounts go back more than a decade to a time when Platner, a combat veteran, says he was struggling with PTSD.
sentences.cn: 一些描述可以追溯到十多年前，当时普拉特纳，一位退伍军人，说他正在与创伤后应激障碍作斗争。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'when...'为定语从句修饰'time'。
sentences.analysis: 主语：Some accounts；谓语：go back；宾语：more than a decade to a time；'when Platner...says he was struggling with PTSD'为定语从句，修饰'time'。

sentences.en: And I've been very up front since the beginning of this campaign that that was a pretty dark period of my life after I came back from my combat service.
sentences.cn: 自从竞选开始，我就一直非常坦诚，那是我服役归来后人生中相当黑暗的一段时期。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，'that...'为宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：I；系动词：'ve been；表语：very up front；'since the beginning of this campaign'为时间状语；'that...'为宾语从句，说明坦诚的内容。

sentences.en: But those serious allegations are just not true.
sentences.cn: 但那些严重的指控根本不属实。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构。
sentences.analysis: 主语：those serious allegations；系动词：are；表语：not true。

sentences.en: The question this morning for Democrats: is it too late to cut bait?
sentences.cn: 今早民主党面临的问题是：现在放弃是否太晚了？
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，疑问句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：it；系动词：is；表语：too late to cut bait；'The question...'为同位语。

sentences.en: I think those are flashing red lights that you have a problematic candidate and that we're -- that Democrats are scrambling right before the Tuesday primary.
sentences.cn: 我认为这些都是警示信号，表明你有一个有问题的候选人，而且我们——民主党人正在周二初选前手忙脚乱。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'that...'为宾语从句，内含并列的定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：I；谓语：think；宾语：those are flashing red lights...；'that you have...'和'that we're...'为并列的定语从句，修饰'flashing red lights'。

sentences.en: His whole entire spiel has been, I was a bad guy. I saw the light.
sentences.cn: 他整个说辞一直是：我曾经是个坏人，我醒悟了。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，表语为直接引语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：His whole entire spiel；系动词：has been；表语：'I was a bad guy. I saw the light.'

sentences.en: And you want more guys like me seeing the light.
sentences.cn: 而你想要更多像我这样的人醒悟。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾宾补结构，'seeing the light'为现在分词短语作宾补。
sentences.analysis: 主语：you；谓语：want；宾语：more guys like me；'seeing the light'为现在分词短语作宾语补足语。

sentences.en: The thing that I think everybody wildly wants to know is do voters abandon this guy because of the problematic behavior?
sentences.cn: 我认为大家都非常想知道的是，选民是否会因为他的问题行为而抛弃他？
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，主语含定语从句，表语为疑问句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：The thing that I think everybody wildly wants to know；系动词：is；表语：do voters abandon this guy because of the problematic behavior?

sentences.en: And a guy who says, Well, in my 20s, I was often drunk and had PTSD, and that is my excuse.
sentences.cn: 而一个说“嗯，我二十多岁时经常喝醉，患有创伤后应激障碍，这就是我的借口”的人。
sentences.structure: 名词短语，'who says...'为定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 中心词：a guy；'who says...'为定语从句，从句中直接引语作宾语。

sentences.en: There are some awful things here with an individual who is clearly struggling with PTSD, depression, alcoholism, and a host of other things.
sentences.cn: 这里有一些可怕的事情，涉及一个明显在与创伤后应激障碍、抑郁、酗酒和许多其他问题作斗争的人。
sentences.structure: There be 句型，'who...'为定语从句。
sentences.analysis: There are some awful things；'with an individual'为介词短语；'who is clearly struggling...'为定语从句修饰'individual'。

sentences.en: But I think, you know, the two folk -- the two categories of people who really need to weigh in are, No. 1, Maine voters. And No. 2, Democratic women.
sentences.cn: 但我认为，你知道，真正需要发表意见的两类人是：第一，缅因州选民；第二，民主党女性。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'who...'为定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：I；谓语：think；宾语：the two categories of people...；'who really need to weigh in'为定语从句修饰'people'。

sentences.en: Like what they are comfortable with defending.
sentences.cn: 比如他们愿意为哪些事情辩护。
sentences.structure: 介词短语，'what...'为宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 介词：Like；宾语：what they are comfortable with defending。

sentences.en: I saw, buried in our clips, Ms. Gillibrand, right, who at one point was at the forefront of weighing in on this kind of drawing a line.
sentences.cn: 我看到，在我们的剪辑中，吉利布兰德女士，她曾一度站在就这种划清界限发表意见的前沿。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'buried in our clips'为插入语，'who...'为定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：I；谓语：saw；宾语：Ms. Gillibrand；'buried in our clips'为过去分词短语作插入语；'who...'为定语从句修饰'Ms. Gillibrand'。

sentences.en: But I do think, when you start with a Nazi tattoo, and then scandal after scandal after scandal comes out, you should really -- we should really be focusing on that first big red flag.
sentences.cn: 但我确实认为，当你从一个纳粹纹身开始，然后一个又一个丑闻曝光时，你真的应该——我们真的应该关注第一个大的警示信号。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'when...'为时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：I；谓语：do think；宾语：you should really...；'when...'为时间状语从句。

sentences.en: If there were a Republican candidate with a Nazi tattoo, with allegations of -- forget the ghosting -- literally grabbing women or being abusive or hostile in relationships, Democrats would lose their minds over that.
sentences.cn: 如果有一个共和党候选人带有纳粹纹身，被指控——忘了突然断联——实际上抓扯女性或在关系中虐待或敌对，民主党人会对此发疯的。
sentences.structure: 虚拟条件句，主句为'would lose'。
sentences.analysis: If引导虚拟条件句，从句：there were a Republican candidate...；主句：Democrats would lose their minds over that。

sentences.en: Business mogul Kevin O'Leary backtracking from plans to build a massive A.I. data center in Utah.
sentences.cn: 商业大亨凯文·奥利里从在犹他州建造大型人工智能数据中心的计划中退缩。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语作主语，'to build...'为不定式短语作定语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：Business mogul Kevin O'Leary；谓语：backtracking；'from plans'为介词短语；'to build...'为不定式短语修饰'plans'。

sentences.en: The letter comes after weeks of protests at public meetings and just days after Utah's government pressured O'Leary to shrink the size of the development.
sentences.cn: 这封信是在数周公众会议抗议之后，以及犹他州政府施压奥利里缩小开发规模几天后发出的。
sentences.structure: 主谓结构，'after...'为时间状语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：The letter；谓语：comes；'after weeks of protests...'为时间状语。

sentences.en: People are concerned about -- about data centers. They're concerned about the lake. They're concerned about resources.
sentences.cn: 人们担心——担心数据中心。他们担心湖泊。他们担心资源。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，'about...'为介词短语作状语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：People；系动词：are；表语：concerned；'about data centers'等为介词短语。

sentences.en: Now, Pelley was fired on Tuesday after a heated staff meeting, where he accused editor-in-chief Bari Weiss of, quote, "murdering '60 Minutes'" after she axed several show staffers and correspondents.
sentences.cn: 周二，佩利在一次激烈的员工会议后被解雇，他在会上指责主编巴里·韦斯在解雇了几名节目工作人员和记者后“谋杀《60分钟》”。
sentences.structure: 主谓结构，'after...'为时间状语，'where...'为定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：Pelley；谓语：was fired；'after a heated staff meeting'为时间状语；'where...'为定语从句修饰'meeting'。

sentences.en: Despite our attempts to engage with Scott Pelley and to find a way back, unfortunately, we weren't able to do so.
sentences.cn: 尽管我们试图与斯科特·佩利接触并找到回归之路，但不幸的是，我们未能做到。
sentences.structure: 让步状语从句，主句为'we weren't able to do so'。
sentences.analysis: Despite引导让步状语从句，从句：our attempts to engage...；主句：we weren't able to do so。

sentences.en: Bari Weiss knows what she said is not true.
sentences.cn: 巴里·韦斯知道她说的不是真的。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'what...'为宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：Bari Weiss；谓语：knows；宾语：what she said is not true。

sentences.en: There was no effort of any kind to, quote, 'find a way back'.
sentences.cn: 没有任何形式的努力，引用一下，“找到回归之路”。
sentences.structure: There be 句型，'to...'为不定式短语作定语。
sentences.analysis: There was no effort；'of any kind'为介词短语；'to find a way back'为不定式短语修饰'effort'。

sentences.en: These have been the most tumultuous eight days in "60 Minutes" history, starting off with the layoffs last week of those top producers and correspondents.
sentences.cn: 这是《60分钟》历史上最动荡的八天，始于上周解雇那些顶级制片人和记者。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，'starting off...'为现在分词短语作状语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：These；系动词：have been；表语：the most tumultuous eight days；'starting off...'为现在分词短语作伴随状语。

sentences.en: She believes the CBS News organization is old-fashioned. She believes "60 Minutes" is out of date and archaic, even though it's really valuable and popular, and she wants to reform it.
sentences.cn: 她认为CBS新闻组织过时了。她认为《60分钟》过时且陈旧，尽管它确实有价值且受欢迎，她想改革它。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'even though...'为让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：She；谓语：believes；宾语：the CBS News organization is old-fashioned；'even though...'为让步状语从句。

sentences.en: He put out a statement last night, actually, a memo to staff, but it was really written for the public, as well.
sentences.cn: 他昨晚实际上发布了一份声明，一份给员工的备忘录，但它实际上也是写给公众的。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'a memo to staff'为同位语。
sentences.analysis: 主语：He；谓语：put out；宾语：a statement；'a memo to staff'为同位语；'but...'为并列句。

sentences.en: I basically view this memo as him making the commitment publicly so that he can keep these remaining correspondents on board and not lose any more staff members.
sentences.cn: 我基本上将这份备忘录视为他公开做出承诺，以便他能留住这些剩下的记者，不再失去更多员工。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾宾补结构，'so that...'为目的状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：I；谓语：view；宾语：this memo；宾补：as him making the commitment publicly；'so that...'为目的状语从句。

sentences.en: But Trump, you know, hangs over this entire conversation, because he sued "60 Minutes"; because he's been critical of "60 Minutes"; and because Paramount has tried to cozy up to Trump while trying to buy CNN and the rest of WBD.
sentences.cn: 但特朗普，你知道，笼罩着整个对话，因为他起诉了《60分钟》；因为他一直批评《60分钟》；还因为派拉蒙在试图收购CNN和WBD其余部分的同时试图讨好特朗普。
sentences.structure: 主谓结构，'because...'为并列原因状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：Trump；谓语：hangs over；宾语：this entire conversation；'because...'为三个并列的原因状语从句。

sentences.en: Ultimately, those corporate politics are inescapably part of this story, even though CBS says it's trying to reform "60 Minutes" because of the culture, not about the politics.
sentences.cn: 最终，那些公司政治不可避免地成为这个故事的一部分，尽管CBS表示它试图改革《60分钟》是因为文化，而不是政治。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，'even though...'为让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：those corporate politics；系动词：are；表语：inescapably part of this story；'even though...'为让步状语从句。

sentences.en: You know, when I watch CBS, I don't think the coverage has changed markedly.
sentences.cn: 你知道，当我观看CBS时，我不认为报道发生了显著变化。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'when...'为时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：I；谓语：don't think；宾语：the coverage has changed markedly；'when I watch CBS'为时间状语从句。

sentences.en: They're going to tune in to "60 Minutes" in September. They're going to sense whether the show is changed or not.
sentences.cn: 他们将在九月收看《60分钟》。他们会感觉到节目是否改变了。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构，'whether...'为宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语：They；谓语：are going to sense；宾语：whether the show is changed or not。

sentences.en: She used her time in that closed-door session where she was sworn under oath in front of Congress as the former attorney general and as Todd Blanche's former boss to say, he ran the whole process, and he was in charge of the whole release, and the Dow's at 50,000, right?
sentences.cn: 她利用在闭门会议上的时间——当时她作为前司法部长和托德·布兰奇的前老板，在国会面前宣誓——说，他负责整个过程，他负责整个发布，而道琼斯指数在50000点，对吧？
sentences.structure: 主句：She used her time... to say...；定语从句：where she was sworn...；并列宾语从句：he ran..., he was in charge..., and the Dow's at...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复杂句，主句为She used her time... to say...，其中包含一个由where引导的定语从句修饰closed-door session，定语从句中又有as短语作状语。say后跟三个并列的宾语从句，最后一个用and连接，并带有反问语气。

sentences.en: There are probably eight senators who are either up for reelection or in their screw it YOLO phase, I'm doing whatever I want or just tough votes that Republicans need the votes of and they can't afford to lose a lot of them.
sentences.cn: 大概有八位参议员要么面临连任，要么处于“管他呢，你只活一次”的阶段，我想做什么就做什么，或者只是艰难的投票，共和党需要他们的选票，而且他们不能失去太多。
sentences.structure: 主句：There are... senators；定语从句1：who are either... or...；定语从句2：that Republicans need...；并列句：and they can't afford...
sentences.analysis: 句子主干为There are... senators，后接who引导的定语从句，其中包含either...or...结构。or后接名词短语tough votes，再跟that引导的定语从句修饰tough votes。最后and连接并列句。

sentences.en: The key for Todd or anybody going through Judiciary Committee is being pretty tight on January the 6th.
sentences.cn: 对托德或任何通过司法委员会的人来说，关键是要在1月6日问题上立场坚定。
sentences.structure: 主系表：The key... is being...；介词短语：for Todd or anybody going through Judiciary Committee
sentences.analysis: 主语为The key，后接for短语说明对象，其中going through...是现在分词短语作后置定语。谓语为is，表语为动名词短语being pretty tight on...。

sentences.en: And I hear from Republican voters who are frustrated with how --
sentences.cn: 我听到共和党选民对……感到沮丧。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾：I hear from... voters；定语从句：who are frustrated with...；介词宾语从句：how...
sentences.analysis: 主句为I hear from Republican voters，后接who引导的定语从句，其中with后跟how引导的宾语从句，但句子被截断。

sentences.en: She can be praising his management and ethics and also throwing him under the bus on a matter that is incredibly popular or unpopular with a bipartisan selection of Americans.
sentences.cn: 她可以赞扬他的管理和道德，同时也可以在一件在两党美国人中都非常受欢迎或不受欢迎的事情上把他推出去当替罪羊。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾：She can be praising... and also throwing...；定语从句：that is... popular or unpopular...
sentences.analysis: 谓语为can be praising... and throwing...，并列两个动名词短语。throwing后接宾语him和状语on a matter，后跟that引导的定语从句修饰matter。

sentences.en: Now, when you start getting into the world of what are the cases I ought to be citing and either fabricating or just pulling information that you as a lawyer have not read, that's a problem.
sentences.cn: 现在，当你开始进入这样一个世界：哪些是我应该引用的案例，以及捏造或只是提取你作为律师没有读过的信息，那就有问题了。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句：when you start...；主句：that's a problem；介词宾语从句：what are the cases...；定语从句：that you... have not read
sentences.analysis: when引导时间状语从句，其中getting into the world of后接what引导的宾语从句，该从句中又包含省略that的定语从句I ought to be citing和and连接的并列结构，最后that引导定语从句修饰information。

sentences.en: At a certain point, you have to own what's happening in the building.
sentences.cn: 在某个时刻，你必须对大楼里发生的事情负责。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾：you have to own；宾语从句：what's happening in the building
sentences.analysis: 主句为you have to own，后接what引导的宾语从句。own在此意为“承认对……负责”。

sentences.en: The idea that this is just about Democrats, no, Republicans have a tough vote on you.
sentences.cn: 认为这只是关于民主党的想法，不，共和党人也要对你进行艰难的投票。
sentences.structure: 同位语从句：The idea that...；并列句：no, Republicans have...
sentences.analysis: The idea后接that引导的同位语从句，说明idea的内容。然后no表示否定，后面是并列句。

sentences.en: And we want to know why ticket prices are changing so dramatically from city to city.
sentences.cn: 我们想知道为什么票价在不同城市之间变化如此之大。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾：we want to know；宾语从句：why ticket prices are changing...
sentences.analysis: 主句为we want to know，后接why引导的宾语从句。

sentences.en: What you really saw was a secretary who was filibustering and upset because I was asking him for receipts.
sentences.cn: 你真正看到的是一个在阻挠议事并感到不安的部长，因为我在向他要收据。
sentences.structure: 主语从句：What you really saw；主系表：was a secretary；定语从句：who was filibustering and upset；原因状语从句：because I was asking...
sentences.analysis: What引导主语从句，谓语为was，表语为a secretary，后接who引导的定语从句，其中包含because引导的原因状语从句。

topics.title: 美国参议院通过700亿美元移民法案
topics.content: 美国参议院经过近20小时的辩论，通过了价值700亿美元的共和党移民法案，旨在为ICE和边境巡逻提供资金至特朗普任期结束。但共和党人拒绝了废除总统反武器化基金的提议。随后，七名共和党参议员投票阻止了重新授权FISA（外国情报监视法）的审议，该法一周后到期。
topics.keywords: Senate, immigration bill, ICE, Border Patrol, FISA, Republican, Democratic

topics.title: 缅因州参议员候选人Graham Platner的丑闻
topics.content: 《纽约时报》报道了缅因州参议员候选人Graham Platner的更多争议，包括与女性的有毒关系及身体威胁行为。Platner否认最严重的指控，但承认曾与PTSD和酗酒作斗争。民主党内部对是否继续支持他存在分歧，而州长Janet Mills仍可能作为替代选项。
topics.keywords: Graham Platner, Maine, Senate candidate, allegations, PTSD, Democratic, #MeToo

topics.title: Kevin O'Leary缩减犹他州AI数据中心项目
topics.content: 商业大亨Kevin O'Leary在公众抗议和州政府压力下，承诺将犹他州AI数据中心项目规模减半，从4万英亩降至2万英亩。公众担忧集中在资源消耗和环境影响上。
topics.keywords: Kevin O'Leary, data center, Utah, AI, public protest, environment

topics.title: CBS《60分钟》节目动荡与Scott Pelley被解雇
topics.content: CBS《60分钟》资深记者Scott Pelley在激烈员工会议后被解雇，他指责主编Bari Weiss“谋杀”该节目。CBS管理层否认政治干预，但节目面临重建，仅剩三名记者。新执行制片人Nick Bilton承诺保持独立性。
topics.keywords: 60 Minutes, Scott Pelley, Bari Weiss, CBS, firing, media, independence

topics.title: AI自我复制风险与监管讨论
topics.content: Anthropic公司警告AI可能很快能够自我复制，引发对AI安全性和监管的讨论。同时，Kevin O'Leary的数据中心项目也凸显了AI发展对资源和环境的影响。
topics.keywords: AI, self-replicate, Anthropic, regulation, data center, safety

topics.title: Epstein文件与司法部任命
topics.content: 前司法部长Pam Bondi在闭门听证中指认代理司法部长Todd Blanche负责Epstein文件的发布，这可能影响Blanche的参议院确认。Epstein案涉及性犯罪和司法透明度，成为两党争议焦点。
topics.keywords: Epstein files, Todd Blanche, Pam Bondi, confirmation hearing, Justice Department

topics.title: AI在法律领域的滥用
topics.content: 律师因使用AI生成虚假案例法而受到法院制裁。AI的“幻觉”导致捏造法律引用，引发职业道德和司法诚信问题。调查显示63%的律师在工作中使用AI，但需警惕其风险。
topics.keywords: AI hallucinations, fake case law, legal ethics, sanctions, fabricating briefs

topics.title: 世界杯与移民执法
topics.content: 洛杉矶县警长表示ICE不会在世界杯比赛期间进行民事移民执法，但保留变更可能。工人和球迷担忧ICE突袭，而国土安全部未明确表态。同时，世界杯门票动态定价引发争议。
topics.keywords: World Cup, ICE, immigration enforcement, SoFi Stadium, dynamic pricing

topics.title: 加州选举计票缓慢
topics.content: 加州因人口众多和邮寄选票规则，计票耗时较长。共和党质疑可能作弊，但民主党强调准确性优先。众议员Kamlager-Dove辩护称系统可靠。
topics.keywords: California vote count, mail-in ballots, election integrity, slow counting

topics.title: 政府腐败与透明度
topics.content: 众议员Kamlager-Dove在听证会上质问国务卿Rubio关于委内瑞拉石油销售资金缺乏透明度的问题，涉及无竞标合同和贿赂基金。她认为这是美国历史上最腐败的政府。
topics.keywords: corruption, transparency, Venezuela oil, slush fund, no-bid contracts
